pattern: Two pointers from 0 and floor(sqrt(c)) close inward, increasing the low square when the sum is too small and decreasing the high square when too large.
hints: If a*a + b*b = c, you can assume a <= b, so b is at most the square root of c.
Start a at 0 and b at floor(sqrt(c)); compare a*a + b*b against c.
Move a up when the sum is below c, move b down when above c, and report success on an exact hit. Stop when a passes b.

edge_inputs.description: perfect square uses 0 and its root
near a perfect square but not a sum of two squares
large value that is not expressible

stress.description: random non-negative integers within the 32-bit signed range
stress.note: two-pointer sweep stays fast even at the upper bound
